title: Duck boat overturns in Cambridge, injuring 11 people
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Duck boat overturned on a boat ramp in Cambridge, injuring 11 people, after a tow rope snapped during a mechanical issue.
The incident occurred around 3 p.m. at 10 Museum Way near Education Street with 31 people aboard the amphibious vehicle.
Boston Duck Tours suspended operations for the day and is working with authorities to investigate the rollover incident.

title: Why JJ Peterka was worth the price of 2 1st-round picks for Bruins
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins acquire JJ Peterka from Utah in exchange for two first-round picks.
Peterka has averaged 26 goals per season over the last three NHL seasons.
Peterka adds speed and offensive skill, addressing key needs identified by Bruins management.

title: MassHealth will stop covering weight-loss drugs next week
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: MassHealth will stop covering GLP-1 and anti-obesity medications for weight loss starting July 1, affecting 22,000 members.
The policy change, part of Gov. Healey's 2027 budget, aims to save $15 million annually amid rising healthcare costs.
GLP-1 drugs like Wegovy, now not covered, cost over $1,300 monthly, posing financial challenges for low-income MassHealth members.

title: Here’s the 7 prospects Bruins added on Day 2 of 2026 NHL Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Bruins traded their 2026 first-round pick to acquire 24-year-old winger JJ Peterka from Utah.
Boston selected two goalies, Yuri Ivanov and Roberto Henriquez, as part of seven picks in the 2026 NHL Draft.
With the 88th pick, the Bruins drafted Nils Bartholdsson, a Swedish winger known for his speed and scoring abilities.

title: Native Venezuelan Willson Contreras speaks on devastating earthquakes: ‘I always keep Venezuela in my heart’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Two devastating earthquakes in Venezuela resulted in over 1,430 deaths and 68,900 missing people, as of Saturday.
Native Venezuelans on the Red Sox, including Willson Contreras, expressed their support by writing messages on their game hats.
Red Sox legend David Ortiz raised awareness and funds for Venezuelan earthquake relief efforts at Fenway Park.

title: Jaylen Brown responds to report that he’d be the 7th-best player on an NBA team according to analytics
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown criticized analytics for discrediting players after being rated as the seventh-best player on an NBA team.
Bobby Marks sparked debate by sharing mixed NBA views on Brown's performance due to negative analytics.
Brown, a top-10 MVP finisher, remains a valuable trade target despite differing team assessments.

title: Red Sox jump on Gerrit Cole early to defeat Yankees in third straight game
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox defeat Yankees 4-1 for third consecutive win with key early hits against Gerrit Cole.
Jake Bennett pitched 6.1 innings for the Red Sox, allowing just three hits to earn his second win.
Masataka Yoshida and Anthony Seigler each hit home runs to boost Boston's offense in the series win.

title: Bruins trade Fabian Lysell to Colorado for forward Ivan Ivan
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins traded Fabian Lysell to the Colorado Avalanche for forward Ivan Ivan.
Ivan Ivan scored 11 goals and 15 assists in 66 AHL games during the 2025-26 season.
Fabian Lysell played 12 NHL games for the Bruins, scoring one goal and two assists.

title: Forget the celebrities in the crowd, France are the entertainers of the 2026 World Cup
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: France won against Norway 4-1 at the 2026 World Cup match in Boston Stadium.
French players performed a remarkable 13-second play from defense to attack, showcasing skill and teamwork.
Despite Norway's higher xG, France's efficient offense confirmed their status as favorites in the tournament.

title: Cape DA investigating alleged threats at Sandwich Middle High
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A school safety app alerted officials to threats at Sandwich Middle High; police are investigating with the DA's office.
The alleged threat involved a student against two classmates, prompting police and school cooperation to ensure safety.
The student has been removed from school, with no return planned for the remaining school year as the investigation continues.
